And I'm not -- I am really not concerned with him closing his eyes and Democrats jumping on a couple of, you know, photographs of his -- of his eyes closed in this setting. This is -- this is theater in this -- in this particular case.
paragraphs.cn: 这 --  -- 这是 --  -- 当然，一切都是公平的，但这是政治作秀。马可·卢比奥说得完全正确。我在白宫时，凌晨两点接到过总统的电话。这个人不睡觉。他的作息时间确实不规律。我并不担心他闭上眼睛，也不担心民主党抓住几张他在这种场合闭眼的照片。这 --  -- 这在这个特定情况下是作秀。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: My grandfather used to say he was just checking his eyelids for holes. I'm just saying. Maybe, you know --
paragraphs.cn: 我祖父常说，他只是在检查眼皮上有没有洞。我只是说说而已。也许，你知道 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: There you go. Still to come here on CNN THIS MORNING, Republicans just passed the immigration funding bill after a nearly 80-day partial government shutdown. What does this do? Did Democrats lose their leverage? Plus.
paragraphs.cn: 就是这样。CNN《今晨》节目接下来，共和党人在政府部分停摆近80天后刚刚通过了移民拨款法案。这意味着什么？民主党是否失去了筹码？另外。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: You had some of Trump's most senior advisors, the top level of the government, gathered in the Situation Room.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普的一些最资深顾问，政府最高层，聚集在战情室。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: The latest excerpts from a forthcoming book revealing how President Trump's closest advisers reportedly tried to manage the Jeffrey Epstein fallout and the release of the president's name.
paragraphs.cn: Sebuah cuplikan terbaru dari buku yang akan segera terbit mengungkapkan bagaimana penasihat terdekat Presiden Trump diduga berusaha menangani dampak kasus Jeffrey Epstein dan terungkapnya nama presiden.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: A nearly $70 billion boost to President Trump's immigration and deportation agenda. Trump signing that bill into law yesterday, which ends now a six-month fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security, which began after the shooting deaths of two U.S. citizens, Alex Pretti and Renee Good. Now that bill provides $38 billion for ICE, $26 billion for Border Patrol and an additional $5 billion for unseen costs. The funding is set to last for the next three years.
paragraphs.cn: Agenda imigrasi dan deportasi Presiden Trump mendapat dana hampir $70 miliar. Trump menandatangani RUU tersebut kemarin, mengakhiri pertarungan enam bulan tentang pendanaan Departemen Keamanan Dalam Negeri. Pertarungan ini dimulai setelah dua warga negara AS, Alex Preti dan Renee Good, ditembak mati. RUU ini menyediakan $38 miliar untuk ICE, $26 miliar untuk Patroli Perbatasan, dan $5 miliar untuk biaya tak terduga. Dana ini akan bertahan selama tiga tahun.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: Keep in mind here, typically it would only be funded for one year at a time. It was also passed without the support of a single Democrat. REP. MIKE JOHNSON (R-LA): The American people are going to remember it. They're going to remember, too, that it was Republicans and Republicans alone who did the responsible thing and funded these critically important agencies at this critical time.
paragraphs.cn: Perlu dicatat, biasanya dana semacam ini hanya dialokasikan setahun sekali. RUU ini juga tidak mendapat dukungan dari Partai Demokrat. Anggota DPR dari Partai Republik Louisiana, Mike Johnson, mengatakan: "Rakyat Amerika akan mengingat ini. Mereka juga akan ingat bahwa Partai Republik, dan hanya Partai Republik, yang melakukan hal yang bertanggung jawab pada saat kritis ini dengan mendanai lembaga-lembaga vital ini."

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: Democrats, on the other hand, demonstrated to the entire country once again how little they prioritize the security of our borders and the livelihoods of those who put their own lives at risk and on the line to defend our homeland.
paragraphs.cn: "Di sisi lain, Partai Demokrat sekali lagi menunjukkan kepada negara betapa mereka tidak peduli terhadap keamanan perbatasan dan penghidupan mereka yang mempertaruhkan nyawa untuk melindungi tanah air."

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: I want to bring in now Democratic Congressman James Walkinshaw of Virginia, who joins us this morning. Nice to have you with us. When we look at where things stand, in terms of this funding, the fact that it was passed for three years, this essentially shields the agencies from the appropriations process. So, what oversight will Congress have over the course of these next three years? REP.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang mari kita hubungi Anggota DPR dari Partai Demokrat Virginia, James Wokinshaw, yang bergabung dengan kami pagi ini. Selamat datang. Ketika kita melihat status dana ini, yang disetujui selama tiga tahun, ini sebenarnya membuat lembaga-lembaga ini tidak terpengaruh oleh proses alokasi. Jadi, bagaimana Kongres akan melakukan pengawasan dalam tiga tahun ke depan?

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: JAMES WALKINSHAW (D-VA): Well, look, I'm certainly hopeful that there'll be a change in control of Congress in January, which would allow us to conduct robust oversight in the form of hearings, subpoenas when necessary, depositions when necessary. But currently, this Republican congress is quite consistent that they're not going to provide real oversight of the reckless, out-of-control mass deportation agenda that we have seen.
paragraphs.cn: James Wokinshaw (Anggota DPR dari Partai Demokrat Virginia) mengatakan: "Ya, saya tentu berharap kendali Kongres akan berubah pada Januari tahun depan sehingga kita dapat melakukan pengawasan yang kuat melalui sidang, panggilan pengadilan, dan pengambilan kesaksian jika perlu. Tapi saat ini, Kongres yang dikuasai Partai Republik sangat kompak, mereka tidak akan melakukan pengawasan nyata terhadap agenda deportasi massal yang ceroboh dan tidak terkendali yang kita lihat."

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: So, I don't know that there will be much oversight for the remainder of this year, but I'm hopeful that in January 2027 we can get some oversight and bring this under control.
paragraphs.cn: "Jadi, saya tidak tahu apakah akan ada banyak pengawasan di sisa tahun ini, tapi saya berharap pada Januari 2027 kita bisa melakukan pengawasan dan mengendalikan situasi."

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: Among the list of demands that Democrats had, we have, of course, limits on face coverings, body camera requirements, clear use of force standards, which led to the record shutdown. Really, none of these demands made it into the final bill. Was it worth it?
paragraphs.cn: Dalam daftar tuntutan yang diajukan Partai Demokrat, termasuk pembatasan masker wajah, pemakaian body camera, standar penggunaan kekuatan yang jelas, dll., yang menyebabkan penutupan pemerintahan yang memecahkan rekor. Faktanya, tidak satu pun dari tuntutan ini dimasukkan dalam RUU final. Apakah itu sepadan?

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: Well, look, what Democrats said is federal law enforcement, ICE, CBP, should operate similarly to our local and state law enforcement, right? Have the same expectations of professionalism that we have of our local police. Republicans, led primarily by one man, Stephen Miller in the White House, said, no way, no how. So, from my perspective, it's always worth it to fight to protect the safety of the American people who were being terrorized by their own government, by masked agents surging into communities.
paragraphs.cn: "Ya, Partai Demokrat berpendapat bahwa lembaga penegak hukum federal, seperti ICE dan CBP, harus beroperasi seperti lembaga penegak hukum lokal dan negara bagian, bukan? Dengan standar profesional yang sama seperti polisi setempat. Dan Partai Republik, yang dipimpin terutama oleh Stephen Miller di Gedung Putih, sangat menentangnya. Jadi, menurut saya, berjuang untuk melindungi keselamatan rakyat Amerika selalu sepadan, mereka diteror oleh pemerintah mereka sendiri, oleh petugas penegak hukum yang memakai masker dan masuk ke komunitas mereka."

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: I think that fight is always worth it. We're going to continue that fight. We're going to have to take it to the ballot box in November. I think the mood and the polling across the country is quite clear that the American people, even those who might have supported the idea of stronger immigration enforcement that Donald Trump ran on, are looking at what they're seeing and saying, this ain't what I voted for. I didn't want to see kids with cancer deported.
paragraphs.cn: "Saya pikir perjuangan ini selalu sepadan. Kami akan terus berjuang. Kami harus menyelesaikan ini di kotak suara pada bulan November. Saya pikir sentimen nasional dan jajak pendapat sangat jelas, rakyat Amerika, bahkan mereka yang mungkin mendukung gagasan penegakan imigrasi yang lebih ketat dari Trump, melihat situasi saat ini dan berkata, ini bukan yang saya pilih. Saya tidak ingin melihat anak-anak penderita kanker dideportasi."

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: I don't want to see pregnant women dragged through the street. I don't want to see American citizens shot dead and killed. The American people know this is out of control. Democrats are going to keep fighting to bring it under control.
paragraphs.cn: "Saya tidak ingin melihat wanita hamil diseret di jalan. Saya tidak ingin melihat warga negara AS ditembak mati. Rakyat Amerika tahu ini sudah di luar kendali. Partai Demokrat akan terus berusaha mengendalikan situasi."

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: Let's talk Epstein if we could. Bill Gates, of course, on Capitol Hill. You sat in that hearing yesterday, listened to his testimony. Representative Burchett, afterwards, came out and told the cameras that he had, in his view, very good coaching. That he had paid for very good coaching is what he said about Bill Gates. Did you find Bill Gates credible?
paragraphs.cn: Mari kita bicara tentang Epstein. Bill Gates tentu saja berada di Capitol Hill. Anda menghadiri sidang itu kemarin dan mendengar kesaksiannya. Anggota DPR Burchett kemudian keluar dan berkata kepada kamera bahwa dia pikir Gates telah menerima pengarahan yang sangat baik. Dia mengatakan Gates membayar untuk pengarahan yang sangat baik. Apakah Anda pikir Bill Gates dapat dipercaya?

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: I did. I did find Bill Gates credible. Everything he said was consistent with what's in the files or in the public record. Look, it is an egregious error in judgment. It's the most charitable way to describe Bill Gates deciding to engage, meet with Jeffrey Epstein even after he knew of Jeffrey Epstein's 2008 guilty plea/conviction for a sex crime. Gates acknowledged that yesterday. He said that was a terrible, terrible mistake, and it certainly was.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya pikir Bill Gates dapat dipercaya. Semua yang dia katakan konsisten dengan dokumen atau catatan publik. Dengar, ini adalah kesalahan penilaian yang serius. Ini adalah deskripsi yang paling lunak tentang keputusan Bill Gates untuk tetap bertemu dan bergaul dengan Jeffrey Epstein setelah mengetahui bahwa Epstein mengaku bersalah/dihukum karena kejahatan seksual pada tahun 2008. Gates mengakuinya kemarin. Dia mengatakan itu adalah kesalahan yang sangat, sangat serius, dan memang begitu.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: When we look at where things stand, Congressman Burchett also calling for transparency, as you know. James Comer, the House Oversight chair, there has been some back and forth on what should be public. But he also talked about who he wanted to call in next. I just want to play that. REP. JAMES COMER (R-KY): I would like for Todd Blanche to come in, in July. Come in and let me -- let me be very clear. Todd Blanche came in a month ago.
paragraphs.cn: Ketika kita melihat situasi saat ini, Anggota DPR Burchett juga menyerukan transparansi, seperti yang Anda tahu. Ketua Komite Pengawas DPR James Comer agak berubah-ubah tentang konten apa yang harus dipublikasikan. Tapi dia juga berbicara tentang siapa yang ingin dia panggil selanjutnya. Saya ingin memutarnya. Anggota DPR dari Partai Republik James Comer: Saya ingin Todd Blanch datang pada bulan Juli. Biarkan dia -- biarkan saya perjelas. Todd Blanch datang sebulan yang lalu.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: He came in with Pam Bondi to answer questions from the Oversight Committee, and the Democrats stormed out. What a missed opportunity for the Democrats. But Todd Blanche answered questions that Republicans had for several hours.
paragraphs.cn: Dia datang bersama Pam Bondi untuk menjawab pertanyaan Komite Pengawas, lalu Partai Demokrat pergi dengan marah. Sungguh kesempatan yang terlewatkan bagi Partai Demokrat. Tapi Todd Blanch menjawab pertanyaan Partai Republik selama berjam-jam.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: I'm curious, both your response to that and also what you feel you would get from Todd Blanche.
paragraphs.cn: Saya ingin tahu, apa tanggapan Anda terhadap ini, dan apa yang Anda pikir bisa didapat dari Todd Blanch?

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: In an effort to help Pam Bondi avoid having to come in and speak under oath and answer questions under oath, Chairman Comer tried to set up a secret, closed-door briefing with Bondi and Todd Blanche. That briefing would have had no or has no transcript. The media, the public would never know. In fact, he just described it. Do you have any idea what they discussed in that meeting? What questions were asked? You don't. It's part of the cover up.
paragraphs.cn: Untuk membantu Pam Bondi menghindari bersaksi di bawah sumpah dan menjawab pertanyaan, Ketua Comer mencoba mengatur pengarahan rahasia tertutup dengan Bondi dan Blanch. Pengarahan itu tidak dicatat. Media dan publik tidak akan pernah tahu. Faktanya, dia baru saja menggambarkannya. Apakah Anda tahu apa yang mereka diskusikan dalam pertemuan itu? Pertanyaan apa yang diajukan? Anda tidak tahu. Ini adalah bagian dari penutupan.

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: And Democrats said, look, we're not going to participate in secret behind door -- behind closed doors briefings as part of this coverup. We want Pam Bondi, under oath, in front of camera so the American people can judge it. And we want Todd Blanche, under oath, in front of cameras and let the American people, not the Oversight Committee members, decide whether they think he's telling the truth or not, about this massive cover up that the Trump administration has been engaged in around the Epstein files.
paragraphs.cn: Partai Demokrat berkata, dengar, kami tidak akan berpartisipasi dalam pengarahan rahasia tertutup yang merupakan bagian dari penutupan ini. Kami ingin Pam Bondi bersaksi di bawah sumpah, di depan kamera, biarkan rakyat Amerika yang menilai. Kami ingin Todd Blanch bersaksi di bawah sumpah, di depan kamera, biarkan rakyat Amerika -- bukan anggota Komite Pengawas -- yang memutuskan apakah mereka pikir dia mengatakan yang sebenarnya dalam penutupan massal yang dilakukan pemerintahan Trump seputar dokumen Epstein.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: We will see if that happens. Certainly a lot of interest and a lot of questions. Congressman Walkinshaw, I appreciate you taking the time this morning. Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: Kita lihat saja nanti. Tentu saja ada banyak minat dan banyak pertanyaan. Anggota DPR Wokinshaw, terima kasih telah meluangkan waktu pagi ini. Terima kasih.

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.text: Thanks for having me.
paragraphs.cn: Terima kasih atas undangannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: While some of Jeffrey Epstein's associates are facing questions on Capitol Hill, we're also learning more about how the White House was attempting to deal with the fallout of this crisis last summer. New reporting from "New York Times" reporter Jonathan Swan and Maggie Haberman details how Trump's top advisers assembled high-level meetings to talk about the growing crisis without the president.
paragraphs.cn: Sementara beberapa rekan Jeffrey Epstein menghadapi interogasi di Capitol Hill, kita juga mengetahui lebih banyak tentang bagaimana Gedung Putih musim panas lalu berusaha menangani dampak krisis ini. Laporan baru dari jurnalis New York Times Jonathan Swan dan Maggie Haberman merinci bagaimana penasihat senior Trump mengadakan pertemuan tingkat tinggi untuk membahas krisis yang semakin memburuk tanpa partisipasi presiden.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: JONATHAN SWAN, WHITE HOUSE REPORTER, "NEW YORK TIMES": So, over the summer of 2025, you had some of Trump's most senior advisors, at the top level of the government, gathered in the Situation Room.
paragraphs.cn: Jurnalis Gedung Putih New York Times Jonathan Swan: Jadi, pada musim panas 2025, beberapa penasihat tingkat tinggi Trump, pejabat tinggi pemerintahan, berkumpul di Situation Room.

paragraphs.speaker: Maggie Haberman
paragraphs.text: Which is typically used for national security or responding to national disasters.
paragraphs.cn: Situation Room biasanya digunakan untuk menangani keamanan nasional atau menanggapi bencana nasional.

paragraphs.speaker: Swan
paragraphs.text: To figure out a way to spin their way out of this.
paragraphs.cn: Untuk mencari cara keluar dari masalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.text: Not with the president, but among themselves. The discussion in the Situation Room was not about how to give these women a hearing or a chance to tell their stories. These meetings were about how to handle the political fallout around the White House.
paragraphs.cn: Bukan dengan presiden, tetapi di antara mereka sendiri. Diskusi di Situation Room bukan tentang bagaimana memberi kesempatan pada para wanita ini untuk menceritakan kisah mereka. Rapat-rapat ini tentang bagaimana menangani konsekuensi politik di sekitar Gedung Putih.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: The group chat is back. Look, it's a fascinating read, an excerpt from their forthcoming book, as we look at the way all of this is playing out. Aaron, one of the many things that stood out to me, it was not only the players that were involved, but how they were responding to these moments in public versus in private. I think Dan Bongino may be the best example. In public, pretty measured. In private, the exact opposite. And had some pretty stark warnings for officials.
paragraphs.cn: Group chat kembali. Lihat, ini bacaan yang menarik, cuplikan dari buku baru mereka yang akan segera terbit, kita lihat bagaimana semua ini terjadi. Aaron, salah satu hal yang membuat saya terkesan bukan hanya peserta yang terlibat, tetapi juga reaksi mereka di depan umum versus di belakang layar. Saya pikir Dan Bongino mungkin contoh terbaik. Di depan umum, cukup terkendali. Di belakang layar, sebaliknya. Dan dia memberikan peringatan yang cukup keras kepada para pejabat.

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.text: Yes, it was pretty remarkable to see some of the quotes from Dan Bongino talking about his superior, who is Attorney General Pam Bondi, and her handling of this. Basically her botched handling of this. Of course, she was out there on TV in the months before talking about, you know, she was asked about the client list. She said, it's on my desk. She handed out those binders that turned out to be rather old information about this. There was very much concern about the politics of this internally.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, melihat beberapa pernyataan Dan Bongino sangat mencolok, dia berbicara tentang atasannya, Jaksa Agung Pam Bondi, dan bagaimana dia menangani masalah ini. Pada dasarnya penanganannya yang buruk. Tentu saja, dia sebelumnya ditanya di TV tentang daftar klien, dan dia berkata, daftarnya ada di meja saya. Dia membagikan map yang ternyata berisi informasi yang sudah usang. Kekhawatiran politik internal tentang hal ini sangat besar.

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.text: And clearly these politics were taken very seriously. One of the things I was really struck by was, these meetings included high level officials at the Justice Department. You remember -- might remember back in the Obama administration there was a big controversy about Attorney General Loretta Lynch meeting with Bill Clinton on the tarmac of an airport during the Hillary Clinton email investigation.
paragraphs.cn: Jelas, masalah politik ini ditangani dengan sangat serius. Salah satu hal yang sangat mengejutkan saya adalah bahwa rapat-rapat ini termasuk pejabat senior Departemen Kehakiman. Anda mungkin ingat pada masa pemerintahan Obama, pertemuan Jaksa Agung Loretta Lynch dengan Bill Clinton di landasan bandara selama penyelidikan email Hillary Clinton menimbulkan kontroversi besar.

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.text: Well, now we have this reporting that basically the Justice Department is strategizing with the White House about how to insulate the president from this budding kind of scandal last year. And it kind of seems par for course for what's been happening in recent years.
paragraphs.cn: Sekarang kita memiliki laporan bahwa Departemen Kehakiman pada dasarnya menyusun strategi dengan Gedung Putih untuk melindungi presiden dari skandal yang mulai muncul tahun lalu ini. Ini tampaknya sejalan dengan apa yang terjadi dalam beberapa tahun terakhir.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: The other thing that struck me in this moment were the involvement of Todd Blanche, Meghan, especially as we were just talking about, right, what could potentially be learned from Todd Blanche if, in fact, there were more questions from the House Oversight Committee.
paragraphs.cn: Hal lain yang membuat saya terkesan adalah keterlibatan Todd Blanche, Megan, terutama yang baru saja kita bicarakan, kan, jika Komite Pengawas DPR benar-benar mengajukan lebih banyak pertanyaan, lalu apa yang mungkin diketahui dari Todd Blanche.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: Given his involvement and his suggestions in those meetings and where he is now and the fact that Pam Bondi, right, was saying, just in the last several days, well, Todd Blanche was responsible for everything related to the release here, it's fascinating the way this all sets it up.
paragraphs.cn: Mengingat partisipasinya dalam rapat-rapat itu dan sarannya, serta posisinya sekarang, dan Pam Bondi baru beberapa hari lalu mengatakan bahwa Todd Blanche bertanggung jawab atas segala sesuatu yang terkait dengan rilis ini, pengaturan ini sungguh menarik.

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: Yes, absolutely. I mean, I think that Blanche is going to have a hard time getting out of committee anyways, and he's going to have to answer some questions about the Epstein files if he wants to get confirmed, regardless of this story that just came out. But I also think, I mean, they said that they talked to over 1,000 people I know for the whole book. But this is a lot of people who are no longer with the administration trying to make themselves look better when they were all at fault.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tentu saja. Maksud saya, saya pikir Blanche akan sulit melepaskan diri dari komite, dan apa pun laporan ini, dia harus menjawab beberapa pertanyaan tentang dokumen Epstein jika dia ingin dikonfirmasi. Tapi saya juga berpikir, mereka mengatakan telah mewawancarai lebih dari 1000 orang untuk buku ini. Tapi banyak dari orang-orang ini sudah tidak lagi menjabat di pemerintahan, mereka mencoba membuat diri mereka terlihat lebih baik padahal semua sama-sama bersalah.

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: This whole handling of this has been disgusting and gross. And I, you know, I've been in the Situation Room for meetings. So has Mike. These are -- that is a sacred place where you have national security conversations. So, the fact that they are talking about how to get Donald Trump out of being in the Epstein files is just gross to me and a complete misuse of those facilities in the White House. But the -- this is a lot of people just trying to make themselves look better in this book that they talk to them.
paragraphs.cn: Seluruh prosesnya menjijikkan dan memuakkan. Dan, Anda tahu, saya pernah menghadiri rapat di Situation Room. Mike juga. Itu adalah tempat sakral untuk dialog keamanan nasional. Jadi, mereka membahas bagaimana membuat Donald Trump terbebas dari pengaruh dokumen Epstein, itu membuat saya muak, penyalahgunaan total fasilitas Gedung Putih ini. Tapi banyak orang dalam buku ini hanya ingin terlihat lebih baik dengan memberikan wawancara.

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: So, the fact that they're even leaking on these meetings that were in the Situation Room is offensive to me.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, fakta bahwa mereka bahkan membocorkan isi rapat Situation Room membuat saya tersinggung.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: So, I think the leaking of the meetings is the other thing I wanted to talk about with you. So, Mike, when you -- when you see that, right, and I should point out, CNN has reached out to both the White House and the DOJ, waiting to hear back. But the fact that there is so much detail in this book, so many quotes, what does that tell you about how this White House is working?
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, saya pikir membocorkan isi rapat adalah hal lain yang ingin saya diskusikan dengan Anda. Mike, ketika Anda melihat ini -- -- Saya harus menunjukkan, CNN telah menghubungi Gedung Putih dan Departemen Kehakiman, dan sedang menunggu tanggapan. Tapi begitu banyak detail, begitu banyak kutipan dalam buku ini, apa yang ini katakan tentang bagaimana Gedung Putih ini beroperasi?

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Well, I'm not sure it tells you anything about how this White Bouse is working. And I think Meghan was spot on, this is a bunch of individuals who have left the administration who are trying to, you know, salvage their reputations. And that's what happens when you get leakers. I mean, there's really three leaks in Washington, one on public policy, you know, issues, one on going after somebody, your political enemy, and the other is to make yourself look better and more important.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya tidak yakin ini memberi tahu Anda bagaimana Gedung Putih ini beroperasi. Saya pikir Megan benar, ini adalah sekelompok orang yang sudah meninggalkan pemerintahan, mencoba menyelamatkan reputasi mereka. Itulah yang terjadi dengan pembocor. Ada tiga jenis kebocoran di Washington: satu tentang masalah kebijakan publik, satu untuk menyerang musuh politik, dan satu lagi untuk membuat diri sendiri terlihat lebih baik dan lebih penting.

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: And I think this is a classic example of that third instance of leakers talking to Swan and Haberman and that wonderful "New York Times" music that they play to make everything seem so serious about what they're revealing in their -- in their things. This is -- this is typical. The White House saw a crisis.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir ini adalah contoh klasik dari jenis kebocoran ketiga, di mana pembocor memberikan informasi kepada Swan dan Haberman, dan musik indah dari New York Times membuat semuanya tampak serius. Ini tipikal. Gedung Putih sedang menghadapi krisis saat itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: They gathered the people together to give their advice to the president, made sure that everyone was on the -- on the same page, or at least aired out what they thought about this, and then went to the president with their decisions. I'm really not seeing anything here that is outstanding or unusual when it comes to the inner workings of a White House.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka mengumpulkan orang untuk memberikan saran kepada presiden, memastikan semua orang setuju, atau setidaknya menyampaikan pemikiran mereka, lalu membawa keputusan kepada presiden. Saya benar-benar tidak melihat sesuatu yang menonjol atau tidak biasa, dalam hal operasi internal Gedung Putih.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: All right. Let's shift, if we could, to the group chat before I let you all go, let's take a break from the politics, people. Aaron, you kick this off because -- I can say kick and it relates directly to what's in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, sebelum kita selesai, mari kita beralih ke group chat, kesampingkan politik dulu. Aaron, kamu mulai -- -- karena saya bilang "mulai" langsung terkait dengan konten group chat-mu.

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.text: Yes, it's a big day. I mean the World Cup's starting. I've been excited about this for a long time. I would encourage people, and we've been talking about this with the people in my life, look at this guy Freddy from Germany. His Twitter feed about his travels through the American south. Experiencing true americana. It will -- it will resurrect your love for this country to see somebody experience these things for the first time.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, hari ini hari besar. Piala Dunia dimulai. Saya sudah menantikannya. Saya mendorong semua orang, kami terus mendiskusikan dengan orang-orang di sekitar, lihat Freddie dari Jerman ini. Tweet-nya mendokumentasikan perjalanannya melintasi Amerika Selatan. Mengalami budaya Amerika yang sesungguhnya. Melihat seseorang mengalami hal-hal ini untuk pertama kalinya akan membangkitkan kembali kecintaan Anda pada negara ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.text: And it will change your perspective about these things that maybe you're starting to take for granted.
paragraphs.cn: Dan itu akan mengubah pandangan Anda tentang hal-hal yang mungkin mulai Anda anggap biasa.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: You got to go to a Buc-ees that is for sure. Meghan, I'm going to go out on a limb here and I'm going to say "Summer House" in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: Kamu harus pergi ke Buc-ees. Megan, saya berani memprediksi, saya akan bilang di group chat-mu "Summer House".

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.text: Of course. Of course. It's all that matters in my life is what's on Bravo. The third installment of "The Reunion" was this week. It was incredible. I think that there's a lot of -- a lot of thoughts back and forth of how people feel. The emotionlessness of Wes and Amanda was incredibly awkward for everyone involved. But, you know, I'm here for more in our new series of "In the City." I'm here for it.
paragraphs.cn: Tentu, tentu. Yang terpenting dalam hidup saya adalah acara Bravo. Episode 3 "Reunion" tayang minggu ini, sangat seru. Saya pikir banyak orang berubah pikiran tentang banyak hal. Sikap dingin Wes dan Amanda membuat semua orang malu. Tapi, Anda tahu, saya menantikan lebih banyak dari seri baru kami "In the City". Saya siap.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: One of these days I'm actually going to watch it, I swear. Mike, you're talking dishwashers. The big debate, to rinse or
paragraphs.cn: Suatu hari nanti saya akan menontonnya, saya janji. Mike, kamu bicara tentang mesin pencuci piring. Perdebatan besar, apakah perlu dibilas dulu atau

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Hill
paragraphs.text: No, you're not supposed to rinse.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, kamu tidak perlu membilas.

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.text: Yes, it's a -- like -- I -- you're not supposed to rinse. Modern dishwashers get the dishes clean. Found that out. But I love this Freddy story. The modern de Tocqueville, walking across the southeast of the United States, exposing what America is really like for Europe. I love it. I love it.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ini seperti -- -- saya -- -- kamu tidak perlu membilas. Mesin pencuci piring modern bisa membersihkan piring. Saya menemukan itu. Tapi saya suka cerita Freddie. Tocqueville modern, berjalan kaki melintasi Amerika Tenggara, menunjukkan kepada Eropa wajah asli Amerika. Saya suka, sangat suka.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: All right, so you're joining in the group chat with Aaron. I love it when we bring people together. Good to see you guys.
paragraphs.cn: Oke, jadi kamu bergabung dengan group chat Aaron. Saya suka kita mengumpulkan orang-orang. Senang bertemu kalian.

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.text: Thanks for joining us. I'm Erica Hill. The headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
